title: gladiolus floribundus subsp. milleri
intro: Gladiolus floribundus subsp. milleri, also known as the Miller's sword lily, is a subspecies of the gladiolus floribundus plant, characterized by its striking white flowers with yellow markings and sword-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. It is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and acting as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to treat various ailments, such as wounds and skin irritations.
